name: Potential Memory Code Injection
notes: This rule triggers when Cobalt strike beacon spawns a Windows process. Cobalt strike beacon spawn a native windows binary and manipulating its memory space resulting in the spawned processes not having commandline arguments specified.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Command (custom) matches any of expressions [WerFault.exe$ or rundll32.exe$ or regsvcs.exe$ or regasm.exe$ or regsvr32.exe$]
TD_list: APPLY Potential Memory Code Injection on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) matches any of expressions [WerFault.exe$ or rundll32.exe$ or regsvcs.exe$ or regasm.exe$ or regsvr32.exe$]
RED_list: Event name: Potential Memory Code Injection

Event description: Detects when Cobalt strike beacon spawns a Windows process. Cobalt strike beacon spawn a native windows binary and manipulating its memory space resulting in the spawned processes not having commandline arguments specified.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
